differentials.name: Surface / juxtacortical osteosarcoma:  4-10%
differentials.raw: Surface / juxtacortical osteosarcoma:  4-10%

differentials.raw: Extraskeletal  4%

differentials.name: Secondary osteosarcoma  5-7% Work-up:   local staging by MR before biopsy; distant staging with bone scan + chest CT Prognosis:   dependent on age, sex, tumor size, site, classification; best predictor is degree of tissue necrosis in postresection specimen following chemotherapy
differentials.raw: Secondary osteosarcoma  5-7% Work-up:   local staging by MR before biopsy; distant staging with bone scan + chest CT Prognosis:   dependent on age, sex, tumor size, site, classification; best predictor is degree of tissue necrosis in postresection specimen following chemotherapy (91% survival with tumor necrosis > 90%, 14% survival with < 90% tumor necrosis)

differentials.raw: slowly growing firm soft-tissue mass; painful + tender (in 25-50%)

differentials.raw: history of trauma (12-31%): in preexisting myositis ossificans / site of intramuscular injection

differentials.raw: history of irradiation (5-10%)

differentials.raw: elevated levels of alkaline phosphatase (prognostic)

differentials.name: multiple local recurrences (in 80-90%) after interval of 2 months to 10 years
differentials.raw: multiple local recurrences (in 80-90%) after interval of 2 months to 10 years

differentials.raw: Osteochondroma (corticomedullary continuity)

differentials.raw: Myositis ossificans, periosteal chondroma, juxtacortical hematoma, fibrous malignancy, periosteal chondrosarcoma, other subtypes of juxtacortical osteosarcomas

differentials.name: Ewing sarcoma
differentials.raw: Ewing sarcoma (rarely periosteal, no perpendicular periosteal reaction, soft- tissue component not mineralized + not low in attenuation + not of very high intensity)

differentials.name: Parosteal osteosarcoma
differentials.raw: Parosteal osteosarcoma (densely ossified juxtacortical mass, 3rd + 4th decade, posterior distal metaphysis of femur, attached to bone by narrow stalk, no perpendicular periosteal reaction)

differentials.name: High-grade surface osteosarcoma
differentials.raw: High-grade surface osteosarcoma (surrounds > 50% of bone circumference, frequent invasion of medullary cavity, no high water content of soft-tissue mass)

differentials.name: Periosteal chondroid tumor
differentials.raw: Periosteal chondroid tumor (well-defined border, typically metaphyseal location, curvilinear peripheral calcifications)

differentials.name: Paget disease (67-90%) ◊  0.2-7.5% of patients with Paget disease develop osteosarcoma dependent on extent of disease
differentials.raw: Paget disease (67-90%) ◊  0.2-7.5% of patients with Paget disease develop osteosarcoma dependent on extent of disease

differentials.name: Sequelae of irradiation
differentials.raw: Sequelae of irradiation (6-22%) 2-40 years ago (malignant fibrous histiocytoma most common; fibrosarcoma 3rd most common) ◊  0.02-4% of patients with radiation therapy develop osteosarcoma related to exposure dose (usually > 1,000 cGy)

differentials.name: Osteonecrosis, fibrous dysplasia, metallic implants, osteogenesis imperfecta, chronic osteomyelitis, retinoblastoma
differentials.raw: Osteonecrosis, fibrous dysplasia, metallic implants, osteogenesis imperfecta, chronic osteomyelitis, retinoblastoma (familial bilateral type)

differentials.name: Ewing sarcoma
differentials.raw: Ewing sarcoma (rare calcifications, cortical thickening + saucerization)

differentials.name: Lymphoma
differentials.raw: Lymphoma (spread outside bone without osseous destruction, calcification uncommon)

differentials.name: Conventional osteosarcoma
differentials.raw: Conventional osteosarcoma

differentials.name: Giant cell tumor
differentials.raw: Giant cell tumor (epiphyseal location, solid mass, isointense to muscle on T1WI)

differentials.name: Lytic metastasis
differentials.raw: Lytic metastasis(no fluid levels)

differentials.name: Chondroblastic conventional osteosarcoma
differentials.raw: Chondroblastic conventional osteosarcoma

differentials.name: Ewing sarcoma, chondrosarcoma, lymphoma
differentials.raw: Ewing sarcoma, chondrosarcoma, lymphoma

context: =  malignant tumor of connective tissue producing osteoid matrix + variable amounts of cartilage matrix + fibrous tissue Most common malignant primary bone tumor in adolescents + children; 2nd most common primary malignant bone tumor after multiple myeloma Prevalence:   4-5÷1,000,000 annually; 15% of all primary bone tumors confirmed at biopsy; < 1% of all cancers in USA Types & Frequency:   underlined are types recognized by WHO ›  high-grade intramedullary  75% ›  telangiectatic  4.5-11% ›  low-grade intraosseous  4-5% ›  small cell  1-4% ›  osteosarcomatosis  3-4% ›  gnathic  6-9% associate
